From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Thailand Standard Time](/w/index.php?title=Thailand_Standard_Time&redirect=no "Thailand Standard Time"))

[Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") follows [Indochina Time](/wiki/UTC%2B07:00 "UTC+07:00"), which is seven hours ahead of [UTC](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). The local mean time in [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok") was originally UTC+06:42:04.[[1]](#cite_note-timeanddate-1) Thailand used this local mean time until 1920, when it changed to Indochina Time, UTC+07:00; ICT is used all year round as Thailand never observed [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time"). Thailand shares the same time zone with [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam"), [Laos](/wiki/Laos "Laos"), [Christmas Island](/wiki/Christmas_Island "Christmas Island"), & [Western Indonesia](/wiki/Time_in_Indonesia "Time in Indonesia"), as well as [parts of Russia](/wiki/Krasnoyarsk_Time "Krasnoyarsk Time").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Thailand&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

* Prior to [1 January 1901](/wiki/New_Year%27s_Day "New Year's Day"), locations in [Siam](/wiki/Siam "Siam") with an [astronomical observatory](/wiki/Time_Zone "Time Zone") would adopt [local mean time](/wiki/Local_mean_time "Local mean time") based on the observatory's geographic position. [Chiang Mai Province](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_Province "Chiang Mai Province") and two other provinces each had an observatory, hence, each province had its own distinct local mean time, with minutes of difference between the three locations.
* On 1 April 1920, the mean time of the [105th meridian east](/wiki/105th_meridian_east "105th meridian east") (passing through [Ubon Ratchathani Province](/wiki/Ubon_Ratchathani_Province "Ubon Ratchathani Province")) was adopted by [Siam](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") as the new [standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time"). The mean time of the 105th meridian is 7 hours ahead of [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") (i.e., local mean time at the [Greenwich Royal Observatory](/wiki/Greenwich_Royal_Observatory "Greenwich Royal Observatory")).
* In July 2001, Thailand announced its intention of moving its time forward one hour, to align with both [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") and [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") and more importantly with [Western Australia](/wiki/Western_Australia "Western Australia"), [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), [Central Indonesia](/wiki/Time_in_Indonesia "Time in Indonesia"), and [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), i.e., [UTC+08:00](/wiki/UTC%2B08:00 "UTC+08:00").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) This was met with much criticism and resistance and the plan was withdrawn.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

| Period in use | Time offset from GMT | Name of time (unofficial) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 January 1880 – 31 March 1920 | [UTC+06:42:04](/wiki/UTC%2B06:30 "UTC+06:30") | Bangkok Mean Time |
| 1 April 1920 – present | [UTC+07:00](/wiki/UTC%2B07:00 "UTC+07:00") | [Indochina Time](/wiki/Indochina_Time "Indochina Time") (ICT) |

## Standardisation of time in Thailand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Thailand&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Standardisation of time in Thailand")]

Thailand declared on 16 March 1920 that people would move their clocks ahead by 17 minutes, 56 seconds on 31 March 1920 to match the time in use in [Southeast Asia](/wiki/Southeast_Asia "Southeast Asia"). The time was switched on 1 April 1920 at 00:00 (old time) to 00:17:56 (new time).[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Timekeeper

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Thailand&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Timekeeper")]

On [1 January 1990](/wiki/New_Year%27s_Day "New Year's Day"), the [Cabinet of Thailand](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Thailand "Cabinet of Thailand") appointed the [Royal Thai Navy](/wiki/Royal_Thai_Navy "Royal Thai Navy") as the official timekeeper for Thailand. Thai Standard Time is derived from five [atomic clocks](/wiki/Atomic_clock "Atomic clock") maintained by the Royal Thai Navy.

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Thailand&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Six-hour clock](/wiki/Thai_six-hour_clock "Thai six-hour clock") used in Thailand
* [Thai lunar calendar](/wiki/Thai_lunar_calendar "Thai lunar calendar")
* [Thai solar calendar](/wiki/Thai_solar_calendar "Thai solar calendar")

Retrieved 2 July 2019.
